q1.title: Can the grid handle more data centers? Is there supply?
q1.verdict: Only with new firm supply: the announced wave is the size of the margin.
q1.stat: 1,500 MW forecast by 2028 vs a 3,629 MW May 2026 system margin
q1.blurb: DICT's 2028 forecast alone equals 41% of the whole system's May margin, and Meralco has committed 1,000 MW for 10 data centers. A data center is near-flat 24/7 load. The operator's own dispatch schedules recorded load curtailed on 101 grid-day(s) in this archive window (5,191.1 MWh). Headroom for a wave this size means new firm supply, not spare change.
q1.src: IEMOP May 2026 report; PCIJ (Meralco commitment); DICT forecast via BusinessWorld; IEMOP RTD regional summaries

q2.title: Is the infrastructure ready? Where would they have to sit?
q2.verdict: The choke points are named, public, and already binding; siting that ignores them inherits them.
q2.stat: 88 pieces of equipment hit limits across the real-time and day-ahead runs on 99 archive days; the Leyte-Luzon HVDC ran at max or offline 69% of Dec 2025
q2.blurb: The inter-island links and named 230 kV corridors already bind routinely; the table lists the receipts by equipment name. Nearly every announced data-center megawatt sits in the Luzon load pocket, on the importing side of those links. One Sual unit (647 MW) equals 18% of the May system margin, which is why a single trip moves the whole grid. Luzon scheduled reserves fell below the stated requirement in at least one 5-minute interval on 62 of the window's days.
q2.src: IEMOP congestions-manifesting files; IEMOP Dec 2025 report; IEMOP outage schedules

q3.title: What would it do to market and retail prices?
q3.verdict: The market already prices the geography daily; the bill follows monthly.
q3.stat: May 2026: P7.79/kWh system (+38.5% vs April); Luzon P7.02, Visayas P10.2, Mindanao P9.28
q3.blurb: One market on paper, three prices in practice: when a link binds, the islands price apart. While WESM ran administered prices (through 2026-05-01), the three grids stayed within P0.015/kWh of each other; once trading resumed the mean daily high-to-low spread across the three grids was P5.88/kWh. Widest daily regional spread in the archive: P15.722/kWh on 2026-06-08. WESM passes into the Meralco generation charge monthly: June carried WESM at P7.0281/kWh inside a P9.0704/kWh generation charge. New flat 24/7 load raises the demand the market clears against in every interval.
q3.src: IEMOP monthly reports; archived LWAP files; Meralco June 2026 advisory
